title: Differential Galois Theory: Nonlinear ODEs & Painlevé

key_theorem.statement: An ODE has the Painlevé property (movable singularities are poles only) iff its movable singularities are EML-2 (Laurent poles = rational divergences). Movable branch points are EML-2 for algebraic exponents, EML-∞ for transcendental. The general solution of a Painlevé-type ODE is EML-∞ (transcendent); special solutions at special parameters are EML-2 or EML-3.

painleve_equations.name: P_I
painleve_equations.special_case: No known elementary or EML-finite solutions. True Painlevé transcendent.
known_solutions: Asymptotic: y ~ ±√(-z/6) as z→-∞ (EML-2); y ~ P(z)/(z-z_0)² near poles (EML-2 Laurent)

painleve_equations.name: P_II
painleve_equations.special_case: α=0: Riccati reduction to Airy equation y'' = zy → EML-3 (Airy Ai, Bi).

painleve_equations.name: P_IV
painleve_equations.special_case: Special parameters: rational solutions y=2z+c (EML-2), Hermite solutions.

painleve_equations.name: P_VI
painleve_equations.special_case: Confluence limits give P_I through P_V. Algebraic solutions for special params → EML-2.

painleve_equations.name: Riccati equation (solvable NL ODE)
painleve_equations.special_case: Riccati reduces to linear ODE if particular solution known → EML-2 general solution.

bernoulli.substitution: v = y^{1-n} → linear ODE for v
bernoulli.reason: Reduces to linear → integral of EML-1/EML-2 → EML-2 solution

abel.solvability: Generally not integrable in elementary functions
special_cases: Constant coefficients: explicit solution via elliptic integrals → EML-∞ generally
abel.reason: Movable branch points in generic case → not Painlevé → EML-∞

nonlinear_kovacic.statement: For an autonomous ODE y' = f(y) with f ∈ EML-k: the solution satisfies EML-depth ≤ k+1 generically. Equality holds when the antiderivative ∫dy/f(y) has no depth reduction. The Painlevé transcendents (P_I–P_VI) are EML-∞ because their nonlinear structure prevents EML-finite integration.

examples.comment: Movable pole at z=C; solution is rational = EML-2
∫dy/(1+y²) = arctan(y) = EML-3; inverse of arctan is tan = EML-3
Logistic equation: solution = sigmoid = EML-2 (rational in e^z)

nonlinear_kovacic.open_question: Is there an EML version of the Painlevé test? I.e., can we predict EML-∞ from the singularity type without solving the ODE?

composition_rules.key_insight: EML depth is SUBadditive under composition: depth(f∘g) ≤ depth(f) + depth(g). Cancellations can reduce depth (e.g., ln∘exp = id: depth 2+1=3 → 0). This is why EML-k forms a filtration, not a grading.

eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Riccati, Bernoulli solutions; rational solutions of Painlevé; movable poles
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Airy solutions (P_II, α=0); Weber parabolic cylinder; elliptic-type specials
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: General Painlevé transcendents (P_I–P_VI); Abel eq generic; y'=f(y) with EML-∞ integral

connections.to_session_59: Session 59 Kovacic for linear ODEs; Session 79 extends to nonlinear
connections.to_session_73: Stokes phenomenon = EML-∞ global; Painlevé = EML-∞ general solution
connections.to_session_66: Complex analysis monodromy; Painlevé monodromy = nonlinear Stokes data
